reviews.review_description: So, the game is good, fun to play with waves lasting just enough to give you that successfulness gratification, doesn't let you get bored of interminable and exhausting never ending waves or unachievable enemies, which is good and enemies get added progressively with different dynamics each one, I think is specially good for families or to show the potential of VR to friends. Is still in an early stage I believe but is fully playable, maybe as the ither comment said, the tutorial could be improved but it adds to the mix haha. Wondering how far this game will go.

For a development in progress this shows a lot of promise. After twenty minutes of madcap wand blasting my firearms are ready to explode! The balance needs a little tweaking and the tutorial a little more tutorialness but this is shaping up really nicely!

This could have been a cute game, but the physics and the shooting design is horrible. 
A real miss.

developer_response.body: Hey Jeff, sorry you didn't like Hordz!  The current game is just a starting point, so we'd be very interested to hear more about what you liked and didn't like so that we can incorporate it into future developments.  Please feel free to email us at language@languagebug.com to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_title: The best game of my life
reviews.review_description:
I like because I like Fighting game
￼
Great fun to play. It gets more challenging every time,  very addictive.

reviews.review_title: Decent for free, but needs polish.
reviews.review_description:
When it comes to a "free" game, I consider whether I feel like my time was well spent. In its current state, no, I don't feel like I'd play this over other VR wave defense shooters.

Pros:
+ Aiming felt pretty dead-on.
+ Different monsters types did add some variety.
+ Menus and stages loaded quickly (other than tutorials, more on that later).
+ The overall vibe was cohesive.

Cons:
- Spamming triggers was too effective of an approach. Some sort of "energy" or reload system would go a long way to forcing a bit of strategy.
- The tutorials kept bugging out. Got stuck in a loop where a tutorial played like 3 times before I could play the related level.
- Map nodes need to be more spread out and ideally have less trees blocking the view. I had a hard time aiming at the specific things I wanted to click on. Adjusting the map tilt to be more top-down instead of quite as isometric could help as well.
- Graphics are not great, but if the core gameplay was ironed out could give them a pass.

Hope to see more from this developer down the road as there are glimmers of potential here!

developer_response.body:
Hi Aviseras, thank you for the detailed feedback!

I've just released version 1.6.1 which addresses some of the issues you mentioned, with more robust tutorials and the map tilted more towards the player.  There's also a new character that's a bit of a departure from the others.

Regarding trigger spamming making the game too easy, it certainly will allow you to survive levels, but if you want to achieve the higher score grades, you'll have to master the art of the "x2" simultaneous kills, which you can't consistently do with button mashing.  Going forward I'll be adding more skill-based ways to achieve even higher scores, starting with higher scores for harder hits in Melee.  Anyway, please take a look at the game when you get a chance, and let us know what you think here, or alternatively email me at language@languagebug.com.

Thank you for playing!
